a nonprofit research foundation

advancing neuroscience, environmental health & stem education.

We are the Xheladin & Xhufe Morina Foundation — funding scholarships, organizing hands-on research, and bringing world-class science education to Kosovo's next generation.

now open · 2026 cohort

braincamp 2026 is here.

Five days of neuroscience, hands-on research, and AI at the Innovation Centre of Kosova. July 20–24. We are only accepting 35 applicants this year — apply before the July 1st deadline.

our mission

three ways we move science forward in kosovo.

The foundation's work spans direct funding, hands-on programs, and original research — each designed to unlock long-term, local capacity.

read the full mission

  1. funds

    Providing scholarships for travel, internships, work experience, and competitions in STEM fields — opening doors that geography alone cannot.

  2. support

    Organizing events and conferences with STEM experts, plus personalized academic mentorship for students preparing for international opportunities.

  3. growth

    Providing research and educational tools — computers, lab equipment, scientific instruments — to strengthen STEM learning environments across Kosovo.

what we do

programs built to last.

01

braincamp

A 5-day intensive neuroscience course taught by PhD students from world-renowned universities like MIT, Harvard, Columbia, and UCL — running since 2018.

explore
02

enri research

Our research arm studying how environmental exposures — air pollution, heat, noise — shape brain health in real time.

explore
03

scholarships

Funding exceptional Kosovar students to attend international programs, competitions, and internships.

explore

dr. egzona morina

founder & principal investigator

from the founder
"i want young people to see science differently — something they can connect with, be excited about, and see themselves in."

After BrainCamp Kosovo's first edition in 2018, what began as a one-week course with UCL PhD students has grown into a foundation supporting hundreds of students across multiple countries.
